notes: No node recovery was needed; all nodes in the batch files had ids.
No import-map artifact was present in the workspace, so no extra cross-batch imports were synthesized.

layers.name: UI
layers.description: App Router pages, client components, and React hooks that shape the dashboard experience.

layers.name: Business
layers.description: Domain services, provider orchestration, config generation, and shared application logic under src/lib.

layers.name: Data
layers.description: Authentication, database access, and API routes that mediate persistence and server-side state.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker, scripts, config, docs, tests, translations, and deployment support assets.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Purpose-built E2E fixture that throws immediately so automated tests can verify the global error boundary path.

nodes.name: E2ECrashPage
nodes.summary: Intentional crash component used only by end-to-end tests to exercise the error boundary rendering flow.

nodes.name: global-error.tsx
nodes.summary: Global client error boundary that renders a themed fatal-error screen with reset and dashboard navigation actions.

nodes.name: GlobalError
nodes.summary: Top-level error boundary component that surfaces a recoverable fallback, exposes the error digest when present, and offers reset navigation.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global Tailwind entry stylesheet defining theme tokens, base typography, custom glass utilities, and motion helpers for the dashboard UI.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root App Router layout that loads locale messages, applies Geist fonts, injects the theme bootstrap script, and wraps the application in the Next Intl provider.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Async root layout component that resolves the active locale and messages before rendering the global HTML shell and skip-link wrapper.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side authentication screen that checks setup status, submits login credentials, and renders localized loading and error states.

nodes.name: LoginPage

nodes.name: not-found.tsx
nodes.summary: Localized 404 page that reuses the public theme toggle and offers a direct path back to the dashboard.

nodes.name: notification-utils.ts

nodes.name: use-focus-trap.ts
nodes.summary: Implements an accessibility focus trap for dialogs and overlays, keeping keyboard focus inside a container while restoring the prior focus on close.
nodes.languageNotes: Attaches a document-level keydown listener and restores the previously focused element during cleanup.

nodes.name: useFocusTrap
nodes.summary: Returns a container ref that traps Tab navigation within the active element and restores focus after the trap unmounts.

nodes.name: use-header-notifications.ts
nodes.summary: Aggregates health, quota, and update state into dismissible header notifications with translation support and per-user persistence.

nodes.summary: Polls the health endpoint and exposes a normalized proxy/database health snapshot with latency and raw payload data.

nodes.name: useHealthStatus
nodes.summary: Uses SWR to refresh service health on an interval and returns boolean health, response latency, raw data, and loading/error flags.

nodes.name: use-proxy-update-check.ts
nodes.summary: Checks for CLIProxyAPIPlus proxy updates, controls update prompt visibility, and triggers the proxy update API for admins.
nodes.languageNotes: Persists dismissed update versions in localStorage so the same release is not repeatedly surfaced.

nodes.name: use-theme.ts

tour.title: Boot the application shell
tour.summary: Start with the root App Router layout to see how locale messages, fonts, the theme bootstrap script, and global providers are established before any page renders.

tour.title: Observe the landing redirect
tour.summary: The public landing route does almost no work; it simply moves visitors into the dashboard, keeping the main surface area behind the authenticated app shell.

tour.title: Compare setup and login flows
tour.summary: The onboarding path creates the first admin account, while the login screen authenticates returning users and gates access based on whether setup is complete.

tour.title: Read auth state from the client
tour.summary: The useAuth hook provides a shared session snapshot for UI components, hiding the SWR fetch/revalidation details behind a simple interface.

tour.title: Trace the server-side auth stack
tour.summary: Session verification, JWT signing, password/rate-limit helpers, and the user DAL form the data boundary that keeps identity decisions off the client.

tour.title: Follow provider management services
tour.summary: Provider orchestration lives in the business layer, where the dashboard syncs provider state, infers provider types, and coordinates OAuth and custom-provider workflows.

tour.title: Study config generation
tour.summary: The config-generator modules transform provider and model selections into OpenCode-compatible output, with shared utilities powering both the full and slim variants.

tour.title: Connect the config UI to the generator pipeline
tour.summary: The config publisher, subscriber, editor, and preview components let users inspect, edit, publish, and resync generated configuration without touching raw files.

tour.title: Understand usage and quota tracking
tour.summary: Quota hooks and the usage collector turn provider usage into refreshable summaries and detailed views, feeding the dashboard’s monitoring and alerting surfaces.

tour.title: See health, notifications, and update prompts
tour.summary: The dashboard combines health polling, notification aggregation, and update checks to keep admins informed without overwhelming them with noise.

tour.title: Inspect resilience and recovery surfaces
tour.summary: Global error handling and the not-found route provide graceful fallback experiences, and the E2E crash fixture shows how the boundary is tested.

tour.title: Close with infrastructure and deployment support
tour.summary: Compose files, Dockerfiles, Prisma migrations, scripts, docs, and translation bundles form the operational shell around the dashboard runtime.
